Home Archive

Why Do React Hooks Rely on Call Order?

React hooks rely on call order because React uses a linked list internally to track hook state.

How useState Works Internally

When you call useState, React looks at the current position in the hooks list. 

function useState(initialValue) {
  const hook = {
    memoizedState: initialValue,
    queue: [],
    next: null
  }
  return [hook.memoizedState, dispatchAction]
}

The Rules of Hooks

React enforces two main rules:

  • Only call hooks at the top level of your component
  • Only call hooks from React function components or custom hooks

Learn more at React documentation.
